description: grandchildren of Japanese-born emigrants
description_wiki: Sansei (三世, "third generation") is a Japanese and North American English term used in parts of the world (mainly in South America and North America) to refer to the children of children born to ethnically Japanese emigrants (Issei) in a new country of residence, outside of Japan.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Yonsei may refer to:

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Wyoming (  wy-OH-ming) is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: city in and county seat of Weber County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Ogden ( OG-dən) is a city in and the county seat of Weber County, Utah, United States, located approximately 10 miles (16 km) east of the Great Salt Lake and 40 miles (64 km) north of Salt Lake City.

description: federal holiday in the United States of America for honoring and mourning the U.S. military personnel who died while serving in the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: Memorial Day is a federal holiday in the United States for mourning the U.S. military personnel who died while serving in the United States Armed Forces.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: World War II Japanese American Internment Museum
description: history museum in McGehee, Arkansas
national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: hamlet in Sacramento County, California, United States
Internment camp for Japanese Americans in Millard County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: The Topaz War Relocation Center, also known as the Central Utah Relocation Center (Topaz) and briefly as the Abraham Relocation Center, was an American concentration camp in which Americans of Japanese descent and immigrants who had come to the United States from Japan, called Nikkei were incarcerated.

description: city in Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Richmond is a city in western Contra Costa County, California, United States.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Berkeley (  BURK-lee) is a city on the eastern shore of San Francisco Bay in northern Alameda County, California, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Salt Lake County, Utah, United States and the capital of Utah
description_wiki: Salt Lake City, often shortened to Salt Lake or SLC, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Utah.

description: international humanitarian movement
description_wiki: The International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement is a humanitarian movement with approximately 16 million volunteers, members, and staff worldwide.

description: United States Armed Forces Training Station
description_wiki: Fort Sill is a major United States Army post north of Lawton, Oklahoma, approximately 85 miles (137 km) southwest of Oklahoma City, established in 1869 during the Indian Wars.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: island in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: New Guinea (Tok Pisin: Niugini; Hiri Motu: Niu Gini; Indonesian: Papua, fossilized Nugini, also known as Papua or historically Irian) is the world's second-largest island, with an area of 785,753 km2 (303,381 sq mi).

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
